name: Lou Reed
abstract: Lewis Allan Reed (March 2, 1942 – October 27, 2013) was an American musician, songwriter and poet. He was the guitarist, singer, and principal songwriter for the rock band the Velvet Underground and had a solo career that spanned five decades.

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: Ron Delsener looks at me and goes, “All right, kid, so you want to do something for the children of the homeless. How would you like Duran-Duran and Lou Reed to do a concert for you at the Beacon Theater... And there I went, and there was Lou Reed singing “Take A Walk On The Wild Side.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Jimmy
about_quotes.evidence: When Lou asked you to tour with him – was that a surprise for you, when Lou Reed . . .?
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Jimmy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
